info.title: Conversation Orchestrator
info.description: Manage configurations, conversations, participants, and communications. Create configurations to define capture rules and channel settings, then use conversations to group related communications.

basic_apikey_or_accountsid.description: This API uses [basic authentication](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-is-basic-authentication). Use an [API key](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iam/api-keys) as the username and the API key secret as the password. You can also use your account SID and auth token, but limit their use to local testing.

X-Conflicting-Resource-Id.description: The ID of the existing resource that conflicts.

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description: Client-generated UUID key to ensure idempotent behavior. Submitting the same key returns the original response without creating a duplicate operation. Keys are scoped to account + region with a 24-hour TTL.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this Conversation was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this Conversation was last updated.

configuration.description: Full configuration settings for this Conversation.

displayName.description: A human-readable name for the configuration. Limited to 32 characters.

description.example: Customer Support Configuration for handling all inbound customer inquiries

conversationGroupingType.description:
Type of Conversation grouping strategy:
- `GROUP_BY_PROFILE`: Groups Communications by resolved Profile from the Memory Store.
  A Profile is looked up or created for `CUSTOMER` Participant types. All Communications from the same Profile are in the same Conversation, regardless of address or channel.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES`: Groups Communications by Participant addresses across all channels.
  A customer using +18005550100 will be in the same Conversation whether they contact by SMS, WhatsApp, or RCS.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES_AND_CHANNEL_TYPE`: Groups Communications by both Participant addresses AND channel.
  A customer using +18005550100 by SMS will be in a different Conversation than the same customer by Voice.

memoryStoreId.description: Memory Store ID for Profile resolution.

channelSettings.description: Channel-specific configuration settings including timeout settings and capture rules.

statusCallbacks.description: List of default webhook configurations applied to Conversations under this Configuration.

intelligenceConfigurationIds.description: List of Intelligence Configuration IDs configured for this Configuration.

memoryExtractionEnabled.description: Whether memory extraction is enabled for conversations under this configuration. Defaults to false.

conversationsV1Bridge.description: Configuration for Conversations V1 bridge. When set, messaging channels route through Conversations V1. Use this to integrate with existing Conversations V1 applications.

type.description: Type of Participant in the Conversation.

profileId.description: Profile ID. Note: This field is only resolved for `CUSTOMER` participant types, not for `HUMAN_AGENT` or `AI_AGENT` participants.

addresses.description:
Communication addresses for this Participant. Address format varies by channel:
- SMS/VOICE: E.164 phone number (such as "+18005550100")
- EMAIL: Email address (such as "user@example.com")
- WHATSAPP: Phone number with whatsapp prefix (such as "whatsapp:+18005550100")
- RCS: Sender ID or phone number with rcs prefix (such as "rcs:brand_acme_agent" or "rcs:+18005550100")

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this Participant was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this Participant was last updated.

address.description:
The address value formatted according to channel type:
- SMS/VOICE: E.164 phone number (such as "+18005550100")
- WHATSAPP: Phone number with whatsapp prefix (such as "whatsapp:+18005550100")
- RCS: Sender ID or phone number with rcs prefix (such as "rcs:brand_acme_agent" or "rcs:+18005550100")
- CHAT: Customer-defined string identifier

content.description: The content of the Communication using type field for discrimination.

resourceId.description: External resource identifier for this Communication (e.g. MessageSid for SMS/RCS/WhatsApp, TranscriptionSid + MessageIndex for Voice). When set, used for Communication deduplication/uniqueness within a Conversation.

deliveryStatus.description: Delivery status of the Communication to this recipient.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this Communication was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this Communication was last updated.

occurredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the communication occurred.

content.text: Hello, I need help with my order

address.description:
The address value formatted according to channel type:
- SMS/VOICE: E.164 phone number (such as "+18005550100")
- WHATSAPP: Phone number with whatsapp prefix (such as "whatsapp:+18005550100")
- RCS: Sender ID or phone number with rcs prefix (such as "rcs:brand_acme_agent" or "rcs:+18005550100")
- CHAT: Customer-defined string identifier

channel.description: Channel type for the Participant address.

participantId.description: Participant ID associated with this address.

address.description:
The address value formatted according to channel type:
- SMS: E.164 phone number (such as "+18005550100")
- WHATSAPP: Phone number with whatsapp prefix (such as "whatsapp:+18005550100")
- RCS: Sender ID or phone number with rcs prefix (such as "rcs:brand_acme_agent" or "rcs:+18005550100")
- CHAT: Customer-defined string identifier

channel.description: Channel type for sending communications. Note: VOICE is receive-only and not supported for send operations.

participantId.description: Optional Participant ID. If omitted, the system will resolve or create the participant based on address and channel.

text.example: Hello, how can I help you today?

example.text: Hello, I need help with my order #12345

text.example: Hello, how can I help you today?

channel.description: Audio channel identifier (0 for inbound, 1 for outbound).

confidence.description: Overall confidence score for the transcription (0.0-1.0).

words.description: Word-level transcription data with timing information.

example.text: Hi I need to check on my order status please

conversations.v2.send_message_participan.description:
Identifies a participant for an Action. Supports three resolution modes:
1. participantId + channel: Resolves address from participant's registered addresses
2. participantId only: Resolves when participant has exactly one address
3. address + channel: Uses explicit address

participantId.description: Participant ID to resolve address from.

address.description: Explicit address formatted according to channel type.

type.description: Action type discriminator. Accepted values: SEND_MESSAGE.

contentId.description:
Content template ID (HX... format). When provided, the template is rendered
with the variables map and sent to the recipient.

variables.description: Variables to substitute into the content template.

mediaUrls.description: URLs of media attachments to include with the message.

type.description: The type of action. Accepted values: SEND_MESSAGE.

status.description:
Current status of the Action.
- PENDING: Action accepted, awaiting downstream confirmation
- COMPLETED: Downstream backend confirmed the action
- FAILED: Downstream backend reported a failure

conversationId.description: The conversation this action belongs to.

related.description:
Named identifiers from downstream. For SEND_MESSAGE:
- messageSid: The downstream message SID (present when PENDING or COMPLETED)
- communicationId: The Communication ID (present when COMPLETED)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the action was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the action was last updated.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the action reached a terminal status.

conversations.v2.communication_endpoint.description:
Endpoint for a communication participant. Supports three resolution modes:

1. **participantId + channel**: Resolves address from participant's registered addresses
2. **participantId only**: Resolves when participant has exactly one address
3. **address + channel**: Uses explicit address (for new recipients or cross-channel)

participantId.description:
Participant ID to resolve address from. When provided, Conversations looks up
the participant's registered addresses and selects based on channel.

address.description:
Explicit address formatted according to channel type:
- SMS/VOICE: E.164 phone number (such as "+18005550100")
- WHATSAPP: Phone number with whatsapp prefix (such as "whatsapp:+18005550100")
- RCS: Sender ID or phone number with rcs prefix (such as "rcs:brand_acme_agent" or "rcs:+18005550100")
- CHAT: Customer-defined string identifier

channel.description: Channel type. Required when participantId has multiple addresses or when using explicit address.

conversations.v2.send_content.description: Content for a send action. Supports text, templates, and media.

contentId.description:
Content template ID (HX... format). When provided, the template is rendered
with the variables map and sent to the recipient.

variables.description: Variables to substitute into the content template. Keys must match placeholders defined in the template.

mediaUrls.description: URLs of media attachments to include with the message.

conversations.v2.channel_settings.description:
Channel-specific parameters forwarded as-is to the downstream sending service.
Allows passing backend-specific fields without requiring API changes.

displayName.description: A human-readable name for the configuration. Limited to 32 characters.

description.description: Human-readable description for the Configuration. Allows spaces and special characters, typically limited to a paragraph of text. This serves as a descriptive field rather than just a name.
description.example: Customer Support Configuration for handling all inbound customer inquiries

conversationGroupingType.description:
Type of Conversation grouping strategy:
- `GROUP_BY_PROFILE`: Groups Communications by resolved Profile from the Memory Store.
  A Profile is looked up or created for `CUSTOMER` Participant types. All Communications from the same Profile are in the same Conversation, regardless of address or channel.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES`: Groups Communications by Participant addresses across all channels.
  A customer using +18005550100 will be in the same Conversation whether they contact by SMS, WhatsApp, or RCS.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES_AND_CHANNEL_TYPE`: Groups Communications by both Participant addresses AND channel.
  A customer using +18005550100 by SMS will be in a different Conversation than the same customer by Voice.

memoryStoreId.description: Memory Store ID for Profile resolution.

channelSettings.description: Channel-specific configuration settings including timeout settings and capture rules.

statusCallbacks.description: List of default webhook configurations applied to Conversations under this Configuration.

intelligenceConfigurationIds.description: A list of Conversational Intelligence configuration IDs.

memoryExtractionEnabled.description: Whether memory extraction is enabled for conversations under this configuration. Defaults to false.

conversationsV1Bridge.description: Configuration for Conversations V1 bridge. When set, messaging channels route through Conversations V1. Use this to integrate with existing Conversations V1 applications.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this Configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this Configuration was last updated.

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking.

example.description: Configuration supporting SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS channels

conversations.v2.configuration_channel_s.description: Channel-specific configuration settings by channel type. Keys should be valid channel types (`VOICE`, `SMS`, `RCS`, `WHATSAPP`, `CHAT`).

captureRules.description: Array of capture rules with from/to addresses and optional metadata. Use `*` for wildcard matching in either direction.

conversations.v2.channel_setting.description: Configuration settings for a specific channel type.

conversations.v2.status_timeouts.description: Timeout settings for channel status transitions.

from.description: The from address. Use `*` for wildcard to match any from address.

to.description: The to address. Use `*` for wildcard to match any to address.

metadata.description: Additional matching criteria for the capture rule. For voice calls, can include `callType` (`PSTN`, `SIP`, and similar).

conversations.v2.capture_rule.description: Defines a capture rule with from and to addresses. Supports wildcard `*` for omnidirectional matching.

conversations.v2.status_callback_config.description: Default webhook configuration for Conversation-level events under this Configuration.

method.description: HTTP method used to invoke the webhook URL.

conversations.v2.patch_conversation_conf.description: Partial configuration update for an existing conversation. Only statusCallbacks can be modified.

statusCallbacks.description: List of webhook configurations for this conversation. Send an empty array to clear all callbacks and stop webhook delivery.

conversations.v2.conversations_v1_bridge.description: Configuration for Conversations V1 bridge. When set, messaging channels route through Conversations V1. Use this to integrate with existing Conversations V1 applications.

serviceId.description: The Conversations V1 Service SID (IS prefix). One configuration per V1 Service SID.

operationId.description: Unique identifier for the long-running operation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the operation was created.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the operation completed. Only present for completed or failed operations.

statusUrl.description: URL to poll for operation status.

error.description: Error details if the operation failed. Follows RFC 9457 Problem Details.

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.description: The HTTP status code for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

related.description:
Named resource identifiers associated with this operation. Keys depend on the operation type:
- config-create, config-update, config-delete: configurationId
- conversation-delete: conversationId

conversations.v2.operation_accepted.description: Slim response for an accepted long-running operation.

statusUrl.description: URL to poll for operation status.

related.description:
Named resource identifiers associated with this operation. Keys depend on the operation type:
- config-create, config-update, config-delete: configurationId
- conversation-delete: conversationId

tags.name: ConversationsV2Configuration
tags.description: A conversation configuration is the top-level object in Conversation Orchestrator. It contains the settings that define how Conversation Orchestrator captures traffic and connects to other services.

tags.name: ConversationsV2Conversation
tags.description: A conversation is a record of interactions between participants. It's the container for all communications that occur during an interaction, including voice calls, SMS messages, and other supported channels.

tags.name: ConversationsV2Participant
tags.description: A participant represents an actor involved in a conversation. Conversation Orchestrator assigns each participant a type that identifies their role, such as customer, human agent, or AI agent.

tags.name: ConversationsV2Communication
tags.description: A communication is the smallest unit of interaction within a conversation. Each communication represents a single event—such as an SMS message or a voice utterance.

tags.name: ConversationsV2Action
tags.description: Perform actions within a Conversation. Actions trigger side effects such as sending messages and return 202 Accepted.

tags.name: ConversationsV2Operation
tags.description: Poll the status of a long-running operation.

- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES`: Groups Communications by Participant addresses across all channels.
  A customer using +18005550100 will be in the same Conversation whether they contact by SMS, WhatsApp, or RCS.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES_AND_CHANNEL_TYPE`: Groups Communications by both Participant addresses AND channel.

delete.description:
Asynchronously delete a conversation and all associated data.
Returns 202 Accepted with an Operation-Id for status tracking via GET /v2/ControlPlane/Operations/{operationId}.

  A Profile is looked up or created for `CUSTOMER` Participant types. All Communications from the same Profile are in the same Conversation, regardless of address or channel.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES`: Groups Communications by Participant addresses across all channels.
  A customer using +18005550100 will be in the same Conversation whether they contact by SMS, WhatsApp, or RCS.
- `GROUP_BY_PARTICIPANT_ADDRESSES_AND_CHANNEL_TYPE`: Groups Communications by both Participant addresses AND channel.
  A customer using +18005550100 by SMS will be in a different Conversation than the same customer by Voice.

memoryStoreId.description: Memory Store ID for Profile resolution.

channelSettings.description: Channel-specific configuration settings including timeout settings and capture rules.

statusCallbacks.description: List of default webhook configurations applied to Conversations under this Configuration.

intelligenceConfigurationIds.description: List of Intelligence Configuration IDs configured for this Configuration.

memoryExtractionEnabled.description: Whether memory extraction is enabled for conversations under this configuration. Defaults to false.

conversationsV1Bridge.description: Configuration for Conversations V1 bridge. When set, messaging channels route through Conversations V1. Use this to integrate with existing Conversations V1 applications.

get.description: Retrieve a list of Participants in a Conversation.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

type.description: Type of Participant in the Conversation.

profileId.description: Profile ID. Note: This field is only resolved for `CUSTOMER` participant types, not for `HUMAN_AGENT` or `AI_AGENT` participants.

addresses.description:
Communication addresses for this Participant. Address format varies by channel:
- SMS/VOICE: E.164 phone number (such as "+18005550100")
- EMAIL: Email address (such as "user@example.com")
- WHATSAPP: Phone number with whatsapp prefix (such as "whatsapp:+18005550100")
- RCS: Sender ID or phone number with rcs prefix (such as "rcs:brand_acme_agent" or "rcs:+18005550100")

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this Participant was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this Participant was last updated.

get.description: Retrieve a list of Communications in a Conversation.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

content.description: The content of the Communication using type field for discrimination.

resourceId.description: External resource identifier for this Communication (e.g. MessageSid for SMS/RCS/WhatsApp, TranscriptionSid + MessageIndex for Voice). When set, used for Communication deduplication/uniqueness within a Conversation.

deliveryStatus.description: Delivery status of the Communication to this recipient.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this Communication was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this Communication was last updated.

occurredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the communication occurred.

get.description:
Retrieve the current status of a long-running operation.
Operations progress through: PENDING -> RUNNING -> COMPLETED or FAILED.

operationId.description: Unique identifier for the long-running operation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the operation was created.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the operation completed. Only present for completed or failed operations.

statusUrl.description: URL to poll for operation status.

error.description: Error details if the operation failed. Follows RFC 9457 Problem Details.

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.description: The HTTP status code for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

related.description:
Named resource identifiers associated with this operation. Keys depend on the operation type:
- config-create, config-update, config-delete: configurationId
- conversation-delete: conversationId
